stem: A manufacturing quality inspection uses 3 parallel inspectors for redundancy. The defect caught requires at least one inspector to function. inspector 1 has a reliability between 78% and 87% (uncertain due to limited operational data). inspector 2 has a reliability of 86%. inspector 3 has a reliability of 91%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all inspectors simultaneously, estimated between 1.8% and 4.3%. What is the overall probability that the defect caught?

ground_truth.answer: P(defect caught) ranges from 0.9543 to 0.9804. Point estimate: 0.9678.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 inspectors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.14 × 0.09 = 0.0028. P(works) = 0.9972.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.13 × 0.14 × 0.09 = 0.0016. P(works) = 0.9984.
With common-cause failure (1.8%-4.3%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9543, 0.9804].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
